description: 'lunoa' is a calendar span historically related to a cycle of the moon. It may refer to that lunar rhythm or to a conventional calendar month, whose length, name, and boundaries come from the calendar being used rather than from the Phi word.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and draw it back into high-back rounded 'u'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and carry nasal airflow into stressed middle-height rounded 'o'. Open the jaw for the separate final central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The shared 'lu' opening and following dental nasal in 'lunoa' and 'lunei' (moon) give the month and the moon an audible kinship without making either word a derivation of the other.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Visible lunar change has supplied a public reference for longer spans in many calendars, even where local month systems differ.

semantic_domains.nature: a span historically related to the moon's recurring visible course
semantic_domains.temporal: a lunar or conventional calendar span between a week and a year
